calculated_metrics.confidence_note: This unit cost is estimated by PRISM by dividing total programme expenditure by the total quantified animal beneficiaries.

details.calculation: ($877,600 USD / 559 total beneficiaries) = $1,569.95 USD per outcome. | A $1,000 USD donation achieves ≈ 0.637 outcomes.

calculated_metrics.name: Cost Per Outcome (USD)

calculated_metrics.confidence_note: IES is a Back-of-the-Envelope Calculation (BOTEC) to standardize impact across interventions and species. It relies on philosophical and empirical assumptions.

details.criteria: Pass: <= 2 years of expenditure. | Warn: 2-5 years. | Fail: > 5 years.
Pass: >= 6 months of operating expenses. | Warn: 3-6 months. | Fail: < 3 months.
Pass: At least one intervention is classified as Tier 1 (Systemic Change) or Tier 2 (Prevention & Scalable Care). | Warn: All verifiable interventions are Tier 3 (Direct Care & Indirect Action).

details.formula: Mapping of verified intervention types to the Intervention Leverage Tier framework.

details.calculation: A quantified counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.criteria: Pass: At least one metric has a quantified counterfactual baseline. | Warn: A qualitative baseline is provided, but lacks quantified data. | Fail: No valid counterfactual baseline is provided.
details.elaboration: Quote: 'As the dog meat farms and slaughterhouses will be forced to shut down, the fallout will leave more than half a million dogs with nowhere to go. Shelters and rescues will bear the burden and strain with little government support, and the lives of these displaced animals hang in the balance without your support of our mission.'
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline with a source quote.

details.criteria: Pass: Government grants are < 80% of total income. | Fail: Government grants are >= 80% of total income.

details.calculation: Operates in a low-neglectedness / saturated area (Companion Animals: 100%).
details.criteria: Pass: >= 50% of beneficiaries are high-neglectedness (farmed/wild). | Warn: 1-49% are high-neglectedness. | Fail: 0% are high-neglectedness.
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

details.calculation: No disclosure of unintended negative impacts found. This is the industry norm.
details.criteria: Bonus: Organisation self-reported on unintended negative impacts. | Not Disclosed: No disclosure found.
details.elaboration: This check rewards epistemic humility. Non-disclosure is standard and not a failure.
details.formula: Check for self-reported unintended consequences or failures.

details.calculation: Organisation provided euthanasia or live-release statistics.
details.criteria: Bonus: Euthanasia/live-release data is disclosed. | Not Disclosed: No disclosure found. | N/A: Not applicable for non-sheltering organisations.
details.elaboration: Quote: 'euthanasia remained a grim but regular outcome, sometimes soaring at 85% of the population.'
details.formula: For direct rescue orgs, check for euthanasia/live-release rate disclosure.

source.quote: 3.2% MANAGEMENT & GENERAL
4.5% FUNDRAISING
92.7% ANIMAL SERVICES (PROGRAM)
TOTAL EXPENSES $946,650
82.8% CONTRIBUTIONS & GRANTS
The government has offered limited subsidies to farmers and restaurant owners, but no support for shelters and rescue organizations
TOTAL REVENUE $993,278
ENDING NET ASSETS $397,624
INTAKE 283
DOGS ADOPTED 276
Team KK9R rescued over 500+ dogs from slaughterhouses, puppy mills, and high-kill shelters, and adopted 276 into loving homes.

As the dog meat farms and slaughterhouses will be forced to shut down, the fallout will leave more than half a million dogs with nowhere to go. Shelters and rescues will bear the burden and strain with little government support, and the lives of these displaced animals hang in the balance without your support of our mission.

metrics.context_qualifier: Guinness World Record for most dogs walked simultaneously by one person

source.quote: This achievement is not just a world record—it is a global reminder of what rescue dogs are capable of when given a chance to thrive.

metrics.metric_name: Dogs participating in Guinness World Record walk

source.quote: A total of 38 Korean K9 Rescue dogs—all survivors of abuse, neglect, or abandonment—walked together in perfect unison

One of the most heartbreaking cases was MacGuiness, an 11-pound Shih Tzu-Poo surrendered through the compassionate teams at Vetco Total Care and Petco in New York City. He arrived in critical condition, with a rubber band tightly wrapped around his ear—an injury the veterinarian believed had been causing him pain for several months. The damage was so severe that it led to necrosis and required immediate amputation.
Over 30 days, we took in four local NYC owner surrenders
the City of Busan officially shut down the puppy mill and both slaughterhouses, marking a significant victory for animal protection in South Korea.

source.quote: The workshop gave our staff the tools to assess and adjust for each dog's emotional needs while building stronger communication between handlers and dogs.
Korean K9 Rescue hosted a five-day professional training workshop at our Bundang Rescue Center in South Korea, which cares for over 140 rescue dogs.
These funds will directly support our mission to reduce suffering and overpopulation through targeted, high-impact veterinary care.
The event raised over $50,000
The event welcomed over 175 guests for an afternoon of connection, celebration, and purpose, all in support of expanding access to spay and neuter services for dogs in need in South Korea.
